# zod-crud

zod-crud is a headless JSON editing engine guarded by Zod schemas. It is not a
UI component library. Apps own rendering, focus, buttons, shortcuts, drag/drop,
and product-specific feature names. zod-crud owns JSON Pointer addressing, JSON
Patch mutation, JSONPath search, schema validation, selection state, clipboard
payload flow, and undo/redo history.

Official site and demos: https://developer-1px.github.io/zod-crud/

## Stable Identity

- Root `zod-crud` is React-free.
- React lives only under `zod-crud/react`.
- Public package imports are `zod-crud` and `zod-crud/react`.
- State, operations, selection snapshots, clipboard payloads, history entries,
  and wire payloads are JSON-serializable.
- Standards vocabulary is intentional: JSON values (RFC 8259), JSON Pointer
  (RFC 6901), JSON Patch (RFC 6902), JSONPath (RFC 9535), Zod validation, and
  W3C-shaped selection naming.

## Import Boundary

Use only package entrypoints:

```ts
import { createJSONDocument } from "zod-crud";
import { useJSONDocument } from "zod-crud/react";
```

Do not import `zod-crud/src/*`, `zod-crud/dist/*`, `application/*`,
`domain/*`, or `foundation/*` subpaths.

## Why / Core / Tutorial Context

Frontend editors usually mutate JSON state. Forms, CMS blocks, kanban boards,
outliners, and settings screens share the same underlying actions: add,
replace, remove, move, duplicate, select, copy, paste, undo, and redo.

zod-crud keeps those editing rules out of UI components. New users do not need
the source layout. They need this flow:

```txt
schema -> document -> pointer/query -> can* -> change -> result
```

Official onboarding should stay outside-in:

1. Concepts: schema, JSON value, Pointer, JSONPath, Patch, can*, Result.
2. Tutorial: build a small card-board editor.
3. API reference: exact methods and types.
4. Maintainer notes: source layout and release checks.

## Task Entrypoints

Prefer the document facade for application code.

| Task | API |
| --- | --- |
| Create a headless document | `createJSONDocument(schema, initial, options?)` |
| Use the same surface in React | `useJSONDocument(schema, initial, options?)` |
| Read current state | `doc.value`, `doc.lastPatch` |
| Read one location | `doc.at(pointer)` |
| List children | `doc.entries(pointer)` |
| Search many locations | `doc.query(jsonPath)` |
| Add, replace, remove, copy, or move values | `doc.patch(...)`, `doc.commit(...)` |
| Check before executing | `doc.canPatch`, `doc.canFind`, `doc.canReplace`, `doc.canRemove`, `doc.canMove`, `doc.canDuplicate`, `doc.canCopy`, `doc.canCut`, `doc.canPaste`, `doc.canPastePayload`, `doc.canUndo`, `doc.canRedo` |
| Duplicate a sibling | `doc.duplicate(pointer, options)` |
| Store selected pointers | `doc.selection` |
| Copy, cut, paste, or paste direct payloads | `doc.clipboard` |
| Undo, redo, merge, or group history | `doc.history` |
| Ask what schema a location accepts | `doc.schema.kind`, `doc.schema.describe`, `doc.schema.accepts` |

`createJSONDocument` and `useJSONDocument` expose the same document surface.
React owns render lifecycle only.

## Minimal Example

```ts
import { z } from "zod";
import { createJSONDocument } from "zod-crud";

const Card = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  title: z.string().min(1),
  status: z.enum(["todo", "doing", "done"]),
});

const doc = createJSONDocument(Card, {
  id: "c1",
  title: "Write docs",
  status: "todo",
}, {
  history: 100,
  selection: true,
});

const patch = [{ op: "replace", path: "/status", value: "doing" }] as const;

if (doc.canPatch(patch).ok) {
  doc.commit(patch, { label: "change status" });
}
```

## Patch, Pointer, JSONPath

Patch paths and selection/clipboard targets are JSON Pointers.

```ts
doc.patch({ op: "replace", path: "/title", value: "Ready" });
doc.patch([
  { op: "replace", path: "/settings/owner", value: "editor" },
  { op: "add", path: "/lists/0/cards/-", value: card },
]);
```

JSONPath is for search only. Use `doc.query(jsonPath)` to get JSON Pointers,
then use those pointers in `doc.patch(...)`.

```ts
const result = doc.query("$..cards[?(@.status=='todo')]");
if (result.ok) {
  doc.patch(result.pointers.map((path) => ({
    op: "replace",
    path: `${path}/status`,
    value: "done",
  })));
}
```

`doc.at(pointer)` returns a `ReadResult`, not the raw value.

```ts
const result = doc.at("/lists/0/cards/0/title");
if (result.ok) result.value;
```

`doc.patch(...)` accepts one operation or an operation array. `doc.commit(...)`
and `doc.canPatch(...)` take operation arrays because they plan or record a
batch.

For burst edits, build one operation array and call `doc.patch(...)` or
`doc.commit(...)` once. `history.transaction` groups history entries, but
repeated `doc.patch(...)` calls still validate repeatedly.

## can* Results

`can*` methods are not booleans. They return reasoned capability results so UI,
tests, and command palettes can share the same disabled reason.

```ts
const can = doc.canPastePayload("/lists/0/cards/-", candidateCard);

if (!can.ok) {
  can.code;
  can.reason;
  can.violations;
}
```

Use the same options in `canPaste(...)` that you will use in
`clipboard.paste(...)`.

## Duplicate

Use `doc.duplicate(pointer, options)` when the intent is sibling duplication,
not raw RFC 6902 `copy`.

```ts
const duplicated = doc.duplicate("/lists/0/cards/0", {
  rekey: { fields: ["id", "slug"], strategy: "suffix" },
});

if (duplicated.ok) {
  duplicated.value;
  duplicated.applied;
}
```

`duplicate`, `clipboard.cut`, `clipboard.paste`, and
`clipboard.pastePayload` mutate immediately. Their `applied` records are for
inspection; do not pass `applied` to `commit` again.

## Selection

Selection is JSON-safe state, not DOM focus. Use selected pointers as explicit
sources for bulk work.

```ts
doc.selection?.selectRanges([
  "/lists/0/cards/0",
  "/lists/0/cards/1",
]);

const source = doc.selection?.selectedPointers ?? [];
doc.clipboard.copy(source);
```

Common surface:

- `selectedPointers`, `primaryPointer`, `anchorPointer`, `focusPointer`, `caret`
- `collapse`, `setBaseAndExtent`, `extend`
- `addRange`, `removeRange`, `toggleRange`, `togglePointer`, `selectRanges`
- `moveCursor`, `extendCursor`, `resolveCursor`
- `textPatch`, `deleteText`
- `snapshot`, `toJSON`, `restore`, `subscribe`

Object members are unordered by the JSON RFC. Prefer explicit pointer lists
over object-member range semantics.

## Clipboard

Clipboard owns copy/cut/paste payload flow. Selection only records what is
selected.

```ts
const copied = doc.clipboard.copy(["/lists/0/cards/0"]);
if (copied.ok) {
  doc.clipboard.paste("/lists/1/cards/-");
}
```

Use a pointer for an insertion position, such as `/items/-` or
`/lists/1/cards/-`. Use `{ before: pointer }`, `{ after: pointer }`, or
`{ replace: pointer }` when the target is an existing value.

```ts
doc.clipboard.pastePayload("/lists/0/cards/-", candidateCard);
doc.clipboard.paste({ after: "/lists/0/cards/0" });
```

Pointer-array copy/cut stores an array payload. If paste should insert each
copied item as a separate array element, pass `{ spread: true, rekey }` to both
`canPaste(...)` and `clipboard.paste(...)`.

## History

History records document patch entries and inverse patches.

```ts
doc.patch({ op: "replace", path: "/title", value: "Final" });
doc.history.undo();
doc.history.redo();
```

For known multi-step changes, prefer one `doc.commit([...])` call with metadata.

```ts
doc.commit(patch, {
  label: "typing",
  origin: "keyboard",
  mergeKey: "title",
  selection: nextSelection,
});

doc.history.mergeLast({ mergeKey: "title" });
```

Use `history.transaction` only when each step must read intermediate document
state.

## Schema Helpers

Use schema helpers to ask what a pointer accepts.

```ts
doc.schema.kind("/lists/0/cards/-", "insert");
doc.schema.describe("/lists/0/cards/-", "insert");
doc.schema.accepts("/lists/0/cards/-", candidateCard, "insert");
```

## Pure Root Helpers

Root helpers are React-free and useful at external JSON boundaries.

```ts
import { applyOperation, applyPatch, applyPatchToTrustedState } from "zod-crud";
```

`applyOperation` and `applyPatch` are pure: same input, same output.
`applyPatch` is an external JSON boundary and checks the whole input state for
JSON safety. Use `applyPatchToTrustedState` only when the caller already owns
that state JSON boundary.

Pointer helpers include `parsePointer`, `tryParsePointer`, `buildPointer`,
`escapeSegment`, `unescapeSegment`, `parentPointer`, `lastSegment`,
`lastSegmentIndex`, `appendSegment`, `withLastSegment`, and `trackPointer`.

## Performance Boundary

For large documents, keep hot UI paths on the document facade: `doc.patch`,
`doc.commit`, and `doc.canPatch`.

Fast document paths apply only for trusted document state plus a plain
structural Zod schema: objects, arrays, records, and scalar validators without
refinements, transforms, or checks. Covered edits include independent non-root
`replace`, root object edits, same-array field, nested-field, and element
`replace` batches, array `add`/`remove`/`copy`/`move`, and same-array `add`/`remove`
batches.

Schemas with `refine`, `superRefine`, transforms, or other checks
intentionally use full root schema validation.

Measure core workloads with:

```sh
npm run perf:core
```

## Tree Editing

Tree semantics are app-owned. zod-crud validates and mutates JSON; apps
translate indent, outdent, visible-row focus, and toolbar commands into JSON
Pointers and JSON Patch operations.

```txt
/nodes/0
/nodes/0/children/0
/nodes/0/children/0/children/0
```

```ts
doc.patch({ op: "add", path: "/nodes/0/children/-", value: node });
doc.patch({ op: "move", from: "/nodes/1", path: "/nodes/0/children/-" });
doc.patch({ op: "move", from: "/nodes/0/children/1", path: "/nodes/1" });
```

For same-array moves, RFC 6902 removes the source first and then adds at the
destination path.

## Public Type Orientation

Important public types include `JSONDocument`, `JSONPatchOperation`,
`JSONPatchInput`, `JSONCapabilityResult`, `JSONResult`, `ReadResult`,
`QueryResult`, `EntriesResult`, `Pointer`, `SelectionState`, `ClipboardState`,
`PasteTarget`, `SchemaState`, `JSONDocumentHistory`,
`JSONDocumentCommitOptions`, `JSONDocumentDuplicateOptions`,
`JSONDocumentPasteOptions`, and `UseJSONDocumentOptions`.

For the complete export list, use `packages/zod-crud/public-contract.json` and
the API reference.

## Maintainer Notes

This section is not required to use the package.

Public source entrypoints:

```txt
src/index.ts   -> zod-crud
src/react.ts   -> zod-crud/react
```

Source layout SSOT:

```txt
src/
├─ index.ts
├─ react.ts
├─ application/
├─ domain/
└─ foundation/
```

Do not create a second public `api/` source layer. Keep public entrypoints at
`src/index.ts` and `src/react.ts`.

Package `prepublishOnly` delegates to the root `release:check`. The root
`release:check` runs `verify`, `perf:core`, and `pack:library`. The root
`verify` includes `docs:evaluate`, which guards README, SPEC, site docs,
`llms.txt`, release notes, public exports, source-layout SSOT, and drift
ledgers.

## Verification

```sh
npm run docs:evaluate
npm run release:check
```

Use `npm run release:check` as the final local gate before publishing.
